examples.title: Student Database with Array of Structures
examples.description: Demonstrates creating, initializing, and searching an array of student structures
examples.code:
STRUCTURE Student
  INTEGER rollNo
  STRING name[50]
  REAL marks
ENDSTRUCTURE

MODULE main()
  DECLARE students[10] AS Student
  DECLARE n = 3, i, searchRoll, found
  
  // Initialize student records
  students[0].rollNo = 101
  students[0].name = "Alice"
  students[0].marks = 85.5
  
  students[1].rollNo = 102
  students[1].name = "Bob"
  students[1].marks = 92.0
  
  students[2].rollNo = 103
  students[2].name = "Charlie"
  students[2].marks = 78.5
  
  // Display all students
  FOR i = 0 TO n-1 DO
    PRINT students[i].rollNo, students[i].name, students[i].marks
  END FOR
  
  // Search by roll number
  PRINT "Enter roll number to search: "
  INPUT searchRoll
  found = -1
  
  FOR i = 0 TO n-1 DO
    IF students[i].rollNo == searchRoll THEN
      found = i
      BREAK
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  IF found != -1 THEN
    PRINT "Found:", students[found].name, students[found].marks
  ELSE
    PRINT "Student not found"
  END IF
END MODULE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the fundamental operations on array of structures: definition, initialization, traversal for display, and linear search for finding a specific record by comparing key fields.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for search, O(1) for access by index
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of elements

examples.title: Employee Management System in C
examples.description: Complete C program for managing employee records using array of structures with sorting functionality
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates array of structures for employee management. It shows initialization at declaration, passing structures to functions, bubble sort for sorting by salary (entire structure swap), and formatted display. The temp = emp[j] swap demonstrates that complete structures can be assigned in C.

examples.title: Array of Structures Equivalent in Python
examples.description: Demonstrates equivalent operations using Python classes and lists to represent array of structures
examples.code:
class Student:
    def __init__(self, roll_no, name, marks):
        self.roll_no = roll_no
        self.name = name
        self.marks = marks

# Array of structures equivalent - list of objects
students = [
    Student(101, "Alice", 85.5),
    Student(102, "Bob", 92.0),
    Student(103, "Charlie", 78.5)
]

# Display all students
print("All Students:")
for s in students:
    print(f"Roll: {s.roll_no}, Name: {s.name}, Marks: {s.marks}")

# Search by roll number
search_roll = 102
found = None
for s in students:
    if s.roll_no == search_roll:
        found = s
        break

if found:
    print(f"\nFound: {found.name} with marks {found.marks}")
else:
    print("\nStudent not found")

# Sort by marks (descending)
students.sort(key=lambda x: x.marks, reverse=True)
print("\nSorted by marks (descending):")
for s in students:
    print(f"{s.name}: {s.marks}")
examples.explanation: Python uses classes to define structure-like templates and lists to hold multiple objects. This code shows the equivalent operations: creating objects with attributes (like structure members), iterating through the list, searching by attribute, and sorting using lambda functions.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for search, O(n log n) for sort

examples.title: Array of Structures Equivalent in Java
examples.description: Shows equivalent implementation using Java classes and arrays for managing student records
examples.explanation: Java implements array of structures using classes (reference types) stored in arrays. Each array element holds a reference to a Student object. This demonstrates object creation, array initialization, access using index notation, method calls on objects, and linear search.
